title: gaillardia aestivalis
intro: Gaillardia aestivalis, also known as the blanket flower, is a perennial plant native to the United States and Canada, characterized by its vibrant yellow or orange flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. Traditionally, Gaillardia aestivalis has been used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its roots and leaves, which contain various flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Gaillardia aestivalis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
